questions.question: Why was living with his grandfather important for Rakesh?
questions.answer: It allowed him to attend school and learn about nature.

questions.question: What does the cherry seed symbolize at the beginning of the story?
questions.answer: A small beginning that can grow into something meaningful.

questions.question: Why did Rakesh plant the cherry seed instead of throwing it away?
questions.answer: His grandfather encouraged him to grow it into a tree.

questions.question: What does the appearance of the twig show about the seed?
questions.answer: That it had begun to grow successfully.

questions.question: What quality did Rakesh show while caring for the plant?
questions.answer: Responsibility and patience.

questions.question: What does the goat eating the leaves represent?
questions.answer: Challenges that occur during growth.

questions.question: Why did Rakesh feel upset when the plant was damaged?
questions.answer: He feared the plant would die.

questions.question: What does the plant surviving the scythe damage show?
questions.answer: The resilience of nature.

questions.question: Why did Rakesh remove the caterpillar from the tree?
questions.answer: To protect the leaves from being eaten.

questions.question: What does the presence of insects on the tree suggest?
questions.answer: That the tree is part of a living ecosystem.

questions.question: Why was the monsoon important for the cherry tree?
questions.answer: The rain helped the tree grow stronger.

questions.question: What does the first blossom symbolize?
questions.answer: Success after patience and care.

questions.question: How did the blossom affect Rakesh emotionally?
questions.answer: It made him proud and happy.

questions.question: Why is the cherry tree important to the story?
questions.answer: It represents the results of patience and nurturing.

questions.question: Why did Rakesh feel connected to the cherry tree?
questions.answer: Because he planted and cared for it himself.

questions.question: What role did the grandfather play in Rakesh’s experience?
questions.answer: He guided and encouraged Rakesh.

questions.question: Why did birds visit the cherry tree later?
questions.answer: To eat the cherries that grew on it.

questions.question: What does the growth of the cherry tree show about time?
questions.answer: That meaningful growth takes years.

questions.question: How does the story show the relationship between humans and nature?
questions.answer: Humans can help nature grow through care and protection.

questions.question: What quality helped Rakesh successfully grow the tree?
questions.answer: Perseverance.

questions.question: Who is the author of the story?
questions.answer: Ruskin Bond.

questions.question: Where is the story mainly set?
questions.answer: In Mussoorie in the Himalayan foothills.

questions.question: Why is patience an important theme in the story?
questions.answer: Because the tree takes years to grow and blossom.

questions.question: What does the title 'Waiting for the Rain' imply?
questions.answer: That growth requires patience and the right conditions.

questions.question: What is the central message of the story?
questions.answer: Care, patience, and perseverance bring meaningful results.
